title: CoDEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: CoDEC tabular-data-resource (TDR) specifications provide set patterns designed make sharing tabular community-level data easier. Examples type data include pediatric hospitalization rate per month per census tract, total number gunshots per season per neighborhood, housing code enforcement density per year per ZIP code. specifications built top Frictionless Tabular Data Resource define CoDEC TDR table data accompanying metadata. See fr package tools curate, read, write frictionless TDRs R. Within {codec} R package, use check_codec_tdr() validate existing TDR CoDEC specifications (version 1.0.0).

title: CoDEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: Data specified average values total counts census tract geographies specific year (year month). required census tract identifer year (year month) columns CoDEC TDR contain spatiotemporal information can used link data.

title: CoDEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: CoDEC TDR must include census tract column named census_tract_id_{year}, {year} replaced decennial vintage census tract geographies used create dataset (.e., census_tract_id_2000, census_tract_id_2010, census_tract_id_2020). census tract identifier column MUST contain 11-digit GEOID identifiers census tracts Hamilton County (GEOID: 39061). list required census tract identifiers 2000, 2010, 2020 available {{cincy}} R package (e.g., cincy::tract_tigris_2010). CoDEC tdr created census tract level link URL (using homepage property) contains code descriptive README file data harmonized (e.g., areal interpolation) census tract geographies.

title: CoDEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: Year (month) temporal variables CoDEC TDR must “tidy format” row represents one observation time. allows cumulatively updating data resources without changing field-specific metadata. CoDEC TDR must include column called year contains integers representing year data collected (e.g., 2018, 2023). also may contain month column, case unique combination year month columns represent calendar month data collected (e.g., “2023” “11” together represent November 2023).

title: CoDEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: Like tabular-data-resources, CoDEC TDR consists directory must contain exactly one data (.csv) file one metadata file named tabular-data-resource.yaml. name directory name CSV file containing data MUST identical name property. example, files must encoded using UTF-8 newlines encoded either \n \r\n. data file must follow RFC 4180 standard CSV files. addition: filename must end .csv first row must header row, containing unique name field value missing, must represented either NA empty string metadata file MUST YAML file named tabular-data-resource.yaml adhering metadata specifications.

title: CoDEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: metadata CoDEC tdr represented hierarchical list specific format. disk, metadata stored separately data tabular-data-resource.yaml file. metadata CoDEC TDR represented hierarchical list properties :

title: CoDEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: property (“metadata property”) named value used describe data resource.

title: CoDEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: schema (“table schema”) special property list information fields (columns) tabular-data-resource.

title: CoDEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: fields (“field descriptors”) special schema property list fields tabular-data-resource, field-specific properties.

title: CoDEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: example CoDEC tdr looks like: CSV data file example CoDEC tdr contain values vintage 2020 census tracts Hamilton County, first last five shown :
code: name: tract_poverty path: tract_poverty.csv title: Fraction of Census Tract Households in Poverty version: 1.2.1 description: |   Measures derived from the 5-year American Community Survey.   Downloaded from (IPUMS NHGIS)[https://nhgis.org) and   converted to match 2020 census tract boundaries. schema:   fields:     census_tract_id_2020:       name: census_tract_id_2020       title: Census Tract Identifier       description: 2020 vintage census tract identifier       type: string     year:       name: year       title: Year       type: number     fraction_poverty:       name: fraction_poverty       title: Fraction of Households in Poverty       type: number year, census_tract_id_2020, fraction_poverty 2020, 39061021508, 0.057 2020, 39061021421, 0.031 2020, 39061023300, 0.030 2020, 39061002000, 0.098 2020, 39061002500, 0.442  ...      ...       ...  2020, 39061021604, 0.259 2020, 39061024700, 0.062 2020, 39061026102, 0.154 2020, 39061023501, 0.046 2020, 39061009800, 0.391

title: Community Data Explorer for Cincinnati
text: Community Data Explorer Cincinnati (CoDEC) data repository composed equitable, community-level data Cincinnati. Data communities live come different spatiotemporal resolutions extents often designed specific goal integrating data. CoDEC defines specifications community-level data effort make FAIR. Operating common data specification means organizations can easily use methods tools harmonizing, storing, accessing, sharing community-level data. data can described, curated, checked CoDEC specifications using {codec} R package. Using tools, collection extant community-level data resources automatically transformed harmonized, community-level tabular data package openly available accompanied (1) richly-documented data catalog, (2) web-based interface exploring learning data, (3) API accessing data scale demand.

title: Community Data Explorer for Cincinnati
text: Like namesake, CoDEC encodes data streams communities live common format (census tract month) can decoded different community-level geographies different time frames. CoDEC relies {cincy} R package define Cincinnati-area geographies interpolate area-level data census tracts, neighborhoods, ZIP codes different years.  initialized CoDEC extant community-level data : Census Tract-Level Neighborhood Indices Harmonized Historical American Community Survey Measures Hamilton County Property Code Enforcement Hamilton County Drive Time CCHMC Hamilton County Land Cover Built Environment Hamilton County Parcel Household Traffic Hamilton County Crime Risk Using specifications, data assembled transparently reproducibly, data structure can automatically validated. saves time resources, leading increased efficiency accelerated innovation. single point data consumption provides ownership process harmonizing data integrates well data governance, importantly, can provide data consumed multiple ways (e.g. dashboard, tabular data file, API). Creating maintaining open community-level data resource equips entire community data-powered decision making boosts organizational trustworthiness. Demonstrating reliability capability appropriately managing shared data helps earn trust organizations communities intended served.

title: Community Data Explorer for Cincinnati
text: White House’s Equitable Data Working Group defined equitable data “allow rigorous assessment extent government programs policies yield consistently fair, just, impartial treatment individuals.” advise equitable data “illuminate opportunities targeted actions result demonstrably improved outcomes underserved communities.” group recommended make disaggregated data norm “… intentional data collected shared, well data protected exacerbate vulnerability members underserved communities, many face heightened risk harm privacy protected.” U.S. Chief Data Scientist, Denice Ross, declared “open data necessary sufficient drive type action need create equitable society.” Open data can fall short driving action equitable. Disaggregating data sensitive attributes, like race ethnicity, can elucidate inequities otherwise remain hidden.

title: Community Data Explorer for Cincinnati
text: Data people sharing data, privacy spectrum tradeoffs risks benefits individuals populations. Data collected individual-level one organization often shared another organization due legal restrictions organization-specific data governance policies. often interested community-level (e.g. neighborhood, census tract, ZIP code) data disaggregated gender, race, sensitive attributes. Achieving data harmonization upstream storage allows contribution disaggregated, community-level data without disclosing individual-level data sharing across organizations.
